recipes.title: Poke
recipes.description: Diced raw ahi tuna marinated with shoyu, sesame oil, sweet onion and limu, a Hawaiian classic.
steps: Cut the ahi into clean 2 cm cubes with a sharp knife and chill until needed.
Slice the sweet onion thinly and the green onion into fine rings.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, salt and chili flakes in a bowl.
Add the tuna, onions and chopped limu to the bowl and fold gently.
Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes to allow the flavours to mingle.
Taste for seasoning and add more soy or salt as needed.
Pile into bowls, sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately over rice.

recipes.title: Kālua Pig
recipes.description: Salt-rubbed pork shoulder cooked low and slow until smoky and shreddable, a luau staple.
steps: Pierce the pork shoulder all over with a sharp knife.
Rub the meat thoroughly with Hawaiian sea salt and liquid smoke.
Line a roasting pan with the ti leaves and place the pork on top.
Pour water into the base of the pan, then wrap completely in more ti leaves.
Cover the pan tightly with foil and roast at 150 C for six hours.
Test that the meat shreds easily with two forks, returning it to the oven if not.
Rest the pork for 20 minutes, then shred and toss with the rendered juices.

recipes.title: Lomi-lomi Salmon
recipes.description: Hawaiian side salad of cured salmon hand-massaged with tomato, sweet onion and green onion.
steps: Soak the salted salmon in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Cut the salmon into very small dice.
Dice the tomatoes and finely chop the sweet onion and green onion.
Combine everything in a bowl and gently massage with your fingers, which is what lomi-lomi means.
Add the ice cubes to keep the mixture cold and crisp.
Season carefully with extra sea salt only if needed.
Chill for 30 minutes before serving on the side of kālua pig.

recipes.title: Loco Moco
recipes.description: Hawaiian comfort plate of white rice topped with hamburger patty, fried egg and rich brown gravy.
steps: Cook the rice and keep warm.
Shape the seasoned mince into four flat patties and fry in a hot pan for three minutes per side.
Remove the patties and saute the diced onion in the same pan until soft.
Sprinkle in the flour and cook for one minute, then whisk in the beef stock, soy and Worcestershire.
Simmer the gravy for five minutes until thickened, finishing with butter.
Fry the eggs in another pan keeping the yolks runny.
Pile rice into bowls, top with a patty, ladle over gravy and crown with a fried egg.

recipes.title: Spam Musubi
recipes.description: Slice of grilled Spam glazed with shoyu sugar sauce, pressed onto rice and wrapped with nori.
steps: Cook the rice and stir through the rice vinegar while still warm.
Slice the Spam into eight 6 mm thick pieces.
Pan fry the slices over medium heat for two minutes per side until lightly crisped.
Combine soy sauce, brown sugar and mirin in the pan and toss the Spam to coat.
Cut nori sheets into strips wide enough to wrap a musubi.
Press warm rice into a musubi mould or rectangular form, top with a slice of Spam.
Wrap a nori strip around the middle, sealing the join with a dab of water.

recipes.title: Haupia
recipes.description: Firm Hawaiian coconut milk pudding, traditionally served as small chilled squares at luaus.
steps: Combine coconut milk, sugar and salt in a saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk cornflour with the water until smooth in a small bowl.
Once the coconut mixture is hot but not boiling, whisk in the cornflour slurry.
Continue whisking constantly until thick and just bubbling, about five minutes.
Pour into a lined 20 cm square tin and smooth the surface.
Cool to room temperature, then chill for at least four hours until completely set.
Cut into small squares and serve cold.

recipes.title: Laulau
recipes.description: Pork and salted butterfish wrapped in young taro leaves and steamed for hours until silken.
steps: Wash the taro leaves carefully and remove the tough stems.
Cut the pork into 4 cm chunks and rub with sea salt.
Cut the butterfish into small pieces.
Stack four to five taro leaves in a hand, place pork and a piece of butterfish in the centre.
Wrap the taro leaves up and around the filling, then wrap a ti leaf around to seal.
Tie each bundle with kitchen twine and arrange in a steamer.
Steam over simmering water for four hours, replenishing water as needed, until pork is tender.

recipes.title: Manapua
recipes.description: Large Hawaiian-Chinese steamed bun filled with sweet char siu pork in a glossy red sauce.
steps: Whisk yeast and sugar with warm water and stand for 10 minutes until foamy.
Combine flour with the yeast mixture and oil to a soft dough, knead for 10 minutes.
Cover and rise in a warm place for 90 minutes until doubled.
Dice the char siu and toss with hoisin, soy, cornflour and a splash of water until thickened in a pan.
Divide the dough into 12 pieces and flatten each into a 12 cm round.
Place a heaped tablespoon of filling in the centre, gather the edges and pinch firmly to seal.
Set seam side down on baking paper squares, prove 30 minutes, then steam for 15 minutes.

recipes.title: Saimin
recipes.description: Hawaiian noodle soup with springy egg noodles in a dashi broth topped with char siu and green onion.
steps: Bring chicken stock to a simmer and stir in dashi powder, soy sauce and grated ginger.
Simmer for 10 minutes for the broth to deepen, then taste and adjust seasoning.
Soft boil the eggs for six minutes, then peel and halve.
Cook the saimin noodles in plenty of boiling water until al dente, drain and divide between bowls.
Slice the char siu thinly and the fish cake into rounds.
Ladle the hot broth over the noodles, then top with char siu, fish cake and a halved egg.
Scatter with finely sliced green onion and serve immediately.
